title: Meet the Artificial Intelligence (AI) Inference Stock That Could Deliver the Biggest Gains Over the Next 3 Years (Hint: It's not Nvidia or Broadcom)
author: Harsh Chauhan
description: As AI inference workloads shift to become the dominant computational workload in data centers, Qualcomm is positioned to capitalize on this trend with its inference-focused chips and attractive valuation. The company projects $15 billion in data center revenue by fiscal 2029 and has secured Meta as a key customer with multi-generation agreements. With earnings expected to grow at 18.5% annually and trading at just 17x forward earnings, Qualcomm stock could potentially reach $473 by fiscal 2029, representing 150% upside.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong growth prospects in AI data center market with $15B revenue target by fiscal 2029, secured Meta partnership with multi-generation agreements, attractive valuation at 17x forward earnings, and projected 18.5% annual EPS growth. Positioned as undervalued compared to Nvidia and Broadcom.

Market leader in AI inference chips with aggressive cost reduction strategy, but stock has delivered only single-digit gains this year despite strong fundamentals. Valuation at 30x earnings and 18.6x sales is considered expensive, limiting near-term upside potential.

Experiencing phenomenal acceleration in revenue and earnings as custom AI chip designer, but stock has delivered only single-digit gains this year. Expensive valuation at 61x earnings and 24x sales significantly limits upside potential despite strong business fundamentals.

Secured as a notable customer for Qualcomm's AI data center chips with multi-generation agreements, positioning itself as a key player in deploying Qualcomm's Dragonfly C1000 server CPU starting in 2026.

title: AI Fears Hit Nebius Stock, But Has the Growth Thesis Changed?
author: Ryan Hasson
description: Nebius stock has pulled back 13% from its June 22 all-time high of $299.86 amid broader AI sector volatility and valuation concerns, trading at a P/S ratio of ~80 and P/E above 93. However, the company's fundamental growth thesis remains intact with strong 2026 revenue guidance of $3-3.4 billion, contracted power capacity over 4 gigawatts, and major multi-year agreements with Meta and Microsoft providing forward visibility. The recent selloff is characterized as a sentiment event rather than a fundamental deterioration.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock faces near-term headwinds from elevated valuation (P/S ~80, P/E >93) and AI sector volatility, with a 13% pullback from peak. However, fundamentals remain strong with robust revenue guidance, major customer contracts (Meta, Microsoft), and accelerating expansion. The article suggests the selloff is sentiment-driven rather than fundamental, making it neutral overall—neither bullish nor bearish on the core business.
Meta is mentioned as one of Nebius's major multi-year agreement partners, providing significant contracted revenue visibility and supporting the AI infrastructure buildout thesis.

Microsoft is highlighted as another major multi-year agreement partner with Nebius, anchoring the company's contracted revenue backlog and demonstrating strong hyperscaler demand for AI infrastructure.

title: The "Magnificent Seven's" Capex Supercycle Has Given Birth to 2 Millionaire-Maker Stocks Hiding in Plain Sight. Here's the Best of the Bunch
author: Harsh Chauhan
description: Major U.S. hyperscalers are projected to spend a record $725 billion on AI infrastructure this year, creating significant opportunities for semiconductor and server suppliers. Micron Technology and Dell Technologies are positioned to benefit from this supercycle, with Micron identified as the better investment due to higher predicted earnings growth and cheaper valuation.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong demand for high-bandwidth memory chips used in AI accelerators, predicted 42% annual HBM market growth through 2030, attractive valuation, and significantly higher earnings growth expected compared to peers.
Booming AI server demand with $51.3 billion backlog, 35% annual market growth predicted through 2034, and accelerating growth rates, though earnings growth expected to be lower than Micron.
Major hyperscaler investing heavily in AI infrastructure as part of the $725 billion combined capex by top four companies.

title: Could the SpaceX, Anthropic, and OpenAI IPOs Trigger a 40% Stock Market Crash? Here's What the Data Says.
author: Micah Zimmerman
description: While a 40% market crash from the SpaceX, Anthropic, and OpenAI IPOs is unlikely, the combined $200 billion raise could trigger significant rotations within AI and tech stocks. Capital funding these IPOs will likely come from existing tech holdings, creating selling pressure on AI infrastructure companies like Nvidia and AMD. The real risk is concentrated valuation pressure on overweighted tech names rather than a systemic market crash.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: SpaceX completed a record $75B IPO but will likely attract capital from existing tech holdings, creating rotation pressure on other AI/tech stocks rather than being a direct negative for the company itself.

Nvidia faces mechanical selling pressure as fund managers rotate capital from AI infrastructure stocks into new IPOs. The article specifically identifies Nvidia as overweighted in QQQ and vulnerable to displacement.
AMD, like Nvidia, is identified as an AI infrastructure company facing rotation pressure and potential selling as capital moves into SpaceX, Anthropic, and OpenAI IPOs.
Microsoft is overweighted in QQQ and could face displacement pressure as new IPOs are added to the index, creating selling pressure on existing constituents.
Apple is overweighted in QQQ and vulnerable to displacement as SpaceX and other mega-cap IPOs enter the index, potentially triggering forced selling.
Alphabet is mentioned as a company that may face doubt about continued AI infrastructure spending, but the article doesn't directly attribute negative pressure to the IPO rotation.
Alphabet is mentioned as a company that may face doubt about continued AI infrastructure spending, but the article doesn't directly attribute negative pressure to the IPO rotation.
Alphabet is mentioned as a company that may face doubt about continued AI infrastructure spending, but the article doesn't directly attribute negative pressure to the IPO rotation.
Alphabet is mentioned as a company that may face doubt about continued AI infrastructure spending, but the article doesn't directly attribute negative pressure to the IPO rotation.
Amazon is mentioned similarly to Alphabet regarding potential AI spending concerns, but no direct negative sentiment is attributed to the IPO rotation.
Meta is overweighted in QQQ and could face displacement pressure as new mega-cap IPOs are added to the index.

QQQ will be forced to add SpaceX and potentially other IPOs, creating index rebalancing effects that displace existing overweighted constituents, but this is a mechanical process rather than a negative outlook for the ETF itself.

title: Overclocking Qualcomm: Rewiring the AI Compute Market
author: Jeffrey Neal Johnson
description: Despite a recent 20% drawdown due to smartphone supply constraints, Qualcomm is strategically transforming from a mobile-dependent chipmaker into a diversified AI compute powerhouse. The company acquired Modular for $3.92 billion to gain software infrastructure and the Mojo programming language, while launching the Dragonfly C1000 server CPU with commitments from Meta and Microsoft. By migrating to 2nm nodes, Qualcomm aims to bring data center-level processing to mobile devices, targeting $40 billion in non-handset revenue by fiscal 2029.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company is executing a strategic transformation from cyclical mobile dependence to diversified AI compute with strong institutional validation (Meta, Microsoft partnerships), robust financial metrics (42% ROE, 22% net margins), and a clear de-risking roadmap targeting $40B non-handset revenue by 2029. Recent selloff presents valuation opportunity.

Meta is mentioned as an anchor client committing to deploy Qualcomm's Dragonfly C1000 servers, validating the architecture but representing a customer relationship rather than direct investment thesis.

Microsoft announced commitment to deploy Qualcomm's High Bandwidth Computing architecture on Azure, serving as institutional validation but representing a supplier relationship rather than direct investment impact.

Apple is mentioned as a competitive reference point with its closed ecosystem, but the article positions Qualcomm's Android strategy as a vendor-neutral alternative rather than directly impacting Apple's business.
